rubric_source: Google Android XR / Material Design for XR developer guidelines

available_lenses.name: Android XR Animator/3D-Professional Specialization
available_lenses.purpose: Rules calibrated for users with a 3D animation/rigging background (Maya, Blender, Unity animation tools), who bring different expectations of precision, control, and manipulation than a general consumer user.

available_lenses.name: Android XR Low-Vision & High-Contrast Accessibility Specialization
available_lenses.purpose: Additional rules focused specifically on users with low vision, color vision deficiency, or light sensitivity, who need larger, higher-contrast, and less detail-dependent UI to use an Android XR app comfortably.

available_lenses.name: Android XR Manufacturing/Industrial AR Specialization
available_lenses.purpose: Rules calibrated for industrial/manufacturing AR users, who typically work hands-busy or gloved, need high physical-alignment precision, and face real-world consequences for interaction errors.

available_lenses.name: Android XR Medical/Clinical Specialization
available_lenses.purpose: Rules calibrated for medical/clinical users, who require extremely low error tolerance, deliberate confirmation for irreversible actions, and often touchless or minimal-contact interaction patterns.

analysis_engine.reason: Set OPENAI_API_KEY to enable AI analysis.

sessions.researcher_summary: This session mostly captured discrete UI actions and did not show enough friction to support a rubric finding.

current_active_lens_names: Android XR Low-Vision & High-Contrast Accessibility Specialization
retrieval_lens_names: Android XR Low-Vision & High-Contrast Accessibility Specialization

retrieved_focus_rules.lens_name: Android XR Low-Vision & High-Contrast Accessibility Specialization
retrieved_focus_rules.rule_text: App layouts should respect and scale correctly with system-level text size and display size settings rather than using fixed, unscalable text and UI elements.
retrieved_focus_rules.why_it_matters: Android XR accessibility settings let users increase text size and display size; an app that ignores these settings leaves low-vision users without their expected accommodation.
retrieved_focus_rules.telemetry_signal: ui_interaction events showing text or layout elements that overlap, clip, or become unreadable when the session metadata indicates a non-default text_scale or display_scale setting.
retrieved_focus_rules.focus_reason: Matched session signals: accessibility, android, interaction, low, non, ui.

retrieved_focus_rules.lens_name: Android XR Low-Vision & High-Contrast Accessibility Specialization
retrieved_focus_rules.rule_text: The app's UI should remain fully legible and functional when the system's High Contrast Fonts or Relumino outline accessibility features are enabled.
retrieved_focus_rules.why_it_matters: These are real user-facing Android XR accessibility settings; if custom app styling overrides or conflicts with them, low-vision users lose the accommodation they turned on.
retrieved_focus_rules.telemetry_signal: Increased hesitation, failed grab_attempts, or long time-to-first-interaction specifically in sessions where session metadata indicates high_contrast_mode or relumino_outline is enabled, compared to sessions without.
retrieved_focus_rules.focus_reason: Matched session signals: accessibility, android, interaction, low, ui, vision.

retrieved_focus_rules.lens_name: Android XR Low-Vision & High-Contrast Accessibility Specialization
retrieved_focus_rules.rule_text: Critical actions and feedback should have a non-visual fallback (audio cue, TTS description, or TalkBack-compatible label) rather than depending on vision alone.
retrieved_focus_rules.why_it_matters: For users with significant vision loss, a purely visual confirmation (for example a subtle color change) is not perceivable at all; an audio or spoken alternative ensures the action is still confirmed.
retrieved_focus_rules.telemetry_signal: Cross-reference press_feedback_confirmation and dead_control_no_response findings from the base rubric against sessions with screen_reader_enabled or low_vision_mode metadata.
retrieved_focus_rules.focus_reason: Matched session signals: android, example, low, non, ui, vision.

retrieved_focus_rules.rule_text: Prioritize highly predictable, consistent interaction patterns over novel or delightful UI flourishes; avoid ambiguous iconography or playful microinteractions for clinical-context controls.
retrieved_focus_rules.why_it_matters: Clinical users need to trust that a control behaves exactly the same way every time under pressure; novelty or ambiguity that might charm a casual user reads as risk in this context.
retrieved_focus_rules.telemetry_signal: Elevated hesitation or repeated verification-style re-checks (for example re-pressing a toggle to confirm state) on controls using non-standard iconography, compared to controls using conventional Material Design patterns.
retrieved_focus_rules.focus_reason: Matched session signals: example, interaction, low, medical, non, ui.

retrieved_focus_rules.rule_text: Where feasible, support touchless or minimal-contact interaction patterns (voice, gaze-assisted selection where available, or air gestures that do not require touching a physical surface).
retrieved_focus_rules.why_it_matters: Sterility requirements and hands-occupied clinical tasks (for example gloved use or holding instruments) make touch-first interaction models impractical or unsafe in this context.
retrieved_focus_rules.telemetry_signal: Sessions relying heavily on grab_attempt/controller_input where hand_pose data suggests limited hand availability (for example one hand consistently unavailable or in a fixed holding position), a strong signal that the current interaction model assumes more hand freedom than the user has.
retrieved_focus_rules.focus_reason: Matched session signals: example, interaction, low, medical.

retrieved_focus_rules.rule_text: System state (especially error states or incomplete actions) must be immediately and unambiguously visible, not inferred from subtle visual cues.
retrieved_focus_rules.why_it_matters: In time-sensitive or high-stakes clinical use, a missed or ambiguous error state can have significant downstream consequences; this persona cannot afford to guess at system state.
retrieved_focus_rules.telemetry_signal: Sessions showing continued interaction attempts on a control after an error/failure state was logged, suggesting the error was not visible or clear enough for the user to notice and adjust.
retrieved_focus_rules.focus_reason: Matched session signals: interaction, low, medical.
